_note: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) vendor-specific JWT claims. Claims already in the IANA JWT Claims Registry (https://www.iana.org/assignments/jwt/jwt.xml) are intentionally excluded.

x5t.description: X.509 certificate SHA-1 thumbprint. Legacy header claim emitted only in v1.0 tokens; functions identically to 'kid' and identifies the signing key.

kid.description: Key ID. Thumbprint of the public key used to validate the token's signature. Emitted in v1.0 and v2.0 tokens.

oid.description: Object identifier. Immutable GUID identifying the user or service principal, unique within a tenant. Two applications signing in the same user receive the same 'oid'. Requires the 'profile' scope.

acct.description: Account type. '0' if the user is a member of the tenant; '1' if the user is a guest.

acrs.description: Authentication context class references. Array of Auth Context IDs the bearer is eligible to perform; used to trigger step-up authentication demands.

aio.description: Azure internal opaque claim used to record data for token reuse. Resources must not use this claim.

app_displayname.description: Display name of the registered application that requested the token.

appid.description: Application (client) ID of the requesting application (v1.0 tokens only). Replaced by 'azp' in v2.0 tokens.

appidacr.description: Application authentication method indicator (v1.0 tokens only). '0' = public client; '1' = client ID and secret; '2' = client certificate. Replaced by 'azpacr' in v2.0 tokens.

idtyp.description: Token type. Value is 'app' for app-only (client credentials) tokens; absent or 'user' for delegated tokens. The most reliable way to distinguish app-only from app+user tokens.

ipaddr.description: IP address of the client that authenticated.

onprem_sid.description: On-premises Active Directory security identifier (SID) of the user. Present when the user has an on-premises account synced via Entra Connect.

platf.description: Device platform indicator. Internal/restricted Microsoft claim; applications must not take a dependency on it.

puid.description: Passport Unique ID. Persistent identifier from Microsoft Accounts (MSA); used to map guest users back to their home-tenant identity.

rh.description: Refresh hash. Opaque internal claim used by Azure to revalidate tokens. Resources must not use this claim.

scp.description: Delegated permission scopes. Space-separated list of OAuth 2.0 scopes granted via user consent. Present only in delegated (user) tokens. Note: Microsoft uses 'scp' where the IANA-registered claim name is 'scope'.

signin_state.description: Sign-in state flags. Array of strings describing the sign-in session; 'kmsi' indicates the user selected 'Keep me signed in'.

tenant_region_scope.description: Geographic region of the resource tenant (e.g., 'EU' for tenants in the EU Data Boundary).

tid.description: Tenant ID. GUID of the Entra ID tenant the user authenticated against. For personal Microsoft accounts the value is always '9188040d-6c67-4c5b-b112-36a304b66dad'.

unique_name.description: Human-readable display identifier for the subject (v1.0 tokens only). Not guaranteed unique within a tenant; for display purposes only.

upn.description: User Principal Name. May be used as a username hint but is not a stable identifier and must not be used as a database key or for authorization decisions.

uti.description: Unique token identifier. Per-token opaque identifier equivalent to the standard 'jti' claim.

ver.description: Token version. '1.0' or '2.0', indicating the Entra ID token format version.

wids.description: Tenant-wide directory role IDs. Array of RoleTemplateID GUIDs representing the Entra built-in directory roles assigned to the user across the entire tenant.

xms_acd.description: Internal Microsoft Entra claim. Opaque restricted value; not intended for use by resource applications.

xms_act_fct.description: Actor facets. Space-separated integer flags describing properties of the token actor (azp/appid); used in Microsoft Entra Agent ID scenarios (e.g., '11' = AgentIdentity).

xms_ftd.description: Agent identity token descriptor. Opaque internal value used in Entra Agent ID token flows; not intended for use by resource applications.

xms_idrel.description: Identity relationship. Space-separated integer flags indicating the relationship between the token subject and the resource tenant (e.g., '1' = member user, '5' = guest, '7' = service principal).

xms_st.description: Subject token object. Internal structured representation of subject identity information; behavior is not stable and is not intended for application use.

xms_sub_fct.description: Subject facets. Space-separated integer flags describing properties of the token subject; used in Entra Agent ID scenarios (e.g., '11' = AgentIdentity, '13' = AgentIDUser).

xms_tcdt.description: Tenant creation date/time. Unix timestamp indicating when the Entra ID tenant was created.

xms_tdbr.description: Tenant data boundary region. Indicates the configured data boundary for the tenant (e.g., 'EU' for EU Data Boundary).

xms_tnt_fct.description: Tenant facets. Space-separated integer flags describing properties of the tenant identified by 'tid'; used in Entra Agent ID scenarios.
